question: Were there any potential events that are not in Pfizer's standard business operations that substantially increased net income in 2019?
gold_answer: Yes, the gain on completion of Consumer Healthcare JV Transaction
predicted: Yes, there was a significant non-standard event in 2019 that substantially increased net income. On July 31, 2019, Pfizer completed a transaction in which it combined its Consumer Healthcare business with GSK's consumer healthcare business into a new joint venture. In exchange, Pfizer received a 32% equity stake in the new company. Upon closing, Pfizer deconsolidated its Consumer Healthcare business and recognized a pre-tax gain of $8.1 billion ($5.4 billion net of tax) in the third quarter of 2019. This gain was recorded in a line item called (Gain) on completion of Consumer Healthcare JV transaction. This was a major one-time event that was not part of Pfizer's standard business operations.
